meta.name: Soul Food Collard Greens
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Slow-simmered collards with smoky pork and vinegar heat. Includes a turkey option.

meta.summary: Tender, slow-simmered collard greens with smoky meat, onion-garlic aromatics, and tangy pepper vinegar potlikker.

notes: If using smoked turkey or pork, reheat to at least 165°F and simmer until greens are tender.
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.

ingredients.label: Smoked meat (ham hock, smoked turkey wing/leg, or smoked neck bones)
Kosher salt (start low; smoked meat/broth can be salty)
Neutral oil (or bacon fat), for sautéing

steps.title: Wash and prep collards
steps.body: Strip collard leaves from thick stems. Stack leaves, roll into a cigar, and slice into 1/2-inch ribbons. Wash thoroughly in a sink/bowl of cold water, swishing to release grit. Lift greens out (don’t pour grit back over), drain well.

steps.title: Sauté onion and garlic
steps.body: Heat oil (or bacon fat) in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and cook 4–6 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Add smoked meat and broth
steps.body: Add smoked meat to the pot, then pour in chicken broth. Add black pepper and bay leaf (optional). Bring to a simmer.

steps.title: Simmer the base for flavor
steps.body: Cover partially and simmer 20 minutes so the broth takes on smoky flavor. Keep at a gentle simmer, not a rolling boil.

steps.title: Add collard greens in batches
steps.body: Add greens a handful at a time, stirring to wilt and make room. Once all greens are in, they should be mostly submerged (add a splash of water/broth if needed).

steps.title: Simmer until tender
steps.body: Partially cover and simmer 45–70 minutes, stirring occasionally, until greens are tender and silky. If the liquid reduces too much, add a little water/broth to keep a soupy potlikker.

steps.title: Shred meat (optional)
steps.body: If using smoked turkey or a hock, remove it, let cool briefly, pull meat from bones, and return meat to the pot. Discard bones/skin as desired.

steps.title: Season and finish with vinegar heat
steps.body: Stir in apple cider vinegar and hot sauce. Add crushed red pepper and brown sugar (optional) if desired. Taste the potlikker and adjust salt at the end (smoked meats vary).

steps.title: Rest briefly, then serve
steps.body: Turn off heat and let sit 10 minutes for flavors to meld. Serve hot with cornbread to soak up the potlikker.
